steps.title: Require both claim parts
steps.content: The claim says Lady Caroline feels bitterness and hides it from her companions. The strongest quotation must show an unpleasant inner state and a contrasting outward appearance at the same time. A merely private feeling is not enough, and an outwardly pleasant surface alone is not enough; the quotation needs both.

steps.title: Find inner and outer contrast
steps.content: A shows annoyance at others' laughter but not clear concealment. B shows irritation about giving orders. C contrasts an indignant stare with seeming docility, but it does not show deeper bitterness. The quotation with cynicism inside and charm outside fits both parts. The answer is D.
